notes: raw_part_labels below are confirmed mappings for the 6 AffordPose categories currently used in dhoi.
Mappings were reviewed by representative-label visualization and GT hand-contact diagnostics on 475 object-affordance entries (2026-04-08).

0.notes: Confirmed by representative visual inspection and GT hand-contact diagnostics. Observed near the bottle top in the inspected Twist/Wrap-grasp previews. Treated as a cap-adjacent closure region for dhoi slot mapping.

5.notes: Confirmed by representative visual inspection and GT hand-contact diagnostics. Dominant bottle body region in the inspected samples. Used as the main wrap-graspable surface.

6.notes: Confirmed by representative visual inspection and GT hand-contact diagnostics. Top-region label co-occurring with Twist in inspected bottle previews. Treated as the primary closure/cap region.

0.notes: Confirmed by representative visual inspection and GT hand-contact diagnostics. Tiny dispenser top patch with limited and mixed GT contact. Kept as UNASSIGNED after GT hand-contact diagnostics because mapping it to a semantic slot slightly hurts dominant-part accuracy.

3.notes: Confirmed by representative visual inspection and GT hand-contact diagnostics. Top actuator/head region nearest to the hand in Press samples. Treated as the main pressable control surface.

5.notes: Confirmed by representative visual inspection and GT hand-contact diagnostics. Large dispenser body region. This is the most natural wrap-grasp surface and is also contacted in Twist samples.

6.notes: Confirmed by representative visual inspection and GT hand-contact diagnostics. Narrow neck/collar region between the body and actuator. It is close to the hand in Twist samples and is used as the twistable closure-like part.

0.notes: Confirmed by representative visual inspection and GT hand-contact diagnostics. Large earphone surface group including liftable buds/loop-like structures. Although not always the closest contact region, it is a valid lift/grasp surface across inspected samples.

4.notes: Confirmed by representative visual inspection and GT hand-contact diagnostics. Secondary earphone region that is often the closest part to the hand in inspected Lift samples. Collapsed into grasp surface as well.

0.notes: Confirmed by representative visual inspection and GT hand-contact diagnostics. Long distal region far from the hand in inspected knife previews (1000/1001). Mapped to the blade / functional end.

1.notes: Confirmed by representative visual inspection and GT hand-contact diagnostics. Compact region nearest to the hand in inspected knife previews (1000/1001). Mapped to the handle / grasp surface.

0.notes: Confirmed by representative visual inspection and GT hand-contact diagnostics. Rare mug sub-region observed mostly in Wrap-grasp GT contacts. Promoted to GRASP_SURFACE after GT hand-contact diagnostics; keeping it graspable is more faithful than leaving it unassigned.

1.notes: Confirmed by representative visual inspection and GT hand-contact diagnostics. Handle-like region closest to the hand in inspected mug Handle-grasp samples. Mapped to grasp surface.

5.notes: Confirmed by representative visual inspection and GT hand-contact diagnostics. Main mug body / wrap-grasp surface in inspected Handle-grasp and Wrap-grasp samples.

7.notes: Confirmed by representative visual inspection and GT hand-contact diagnostics. Rare mug region observed in Support samples; appears to cover a broad support/base contact surface. Collapsed into grasp surface as the closest dhoi slot.

0.notes: Confirmed by representative visual inspection and GT hand-contact diagnostics. Thin distal blade region far from the hand in inspected scissors previews (10412/10429/10483). Mapped to functional end.

1.notes: Confirmed by representative visual inspection and GT hand-contact diagnostics. Handle-loop region nearest to the hand in inspected scissors previews. Mapped to grasp surface.

9.notes: Confirmed by representative visual inspection and GT hand-contact diagnostics. Tiny rare region seen only in scissors_10483, likely a hinge/connector sub-part. Left unassigned rather than forcing it into a dhoi slot.
